summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting on March 26, 2024. Key topics included the resignation of two full-time firefighter/paramedics to part-time status due to uncompetitive pay, authorization to issue an RFP for a construction manager for Station 18, discussion of property insurance appraisal discrepancies (particularly Station 19's valuation), and authorization to negotiate with a new IT company that could save approximately $19,000 annually. The board also discussed the failed fire levy and plans to place it on the November ballot.
decisions: Approved payment of bills for March 13–26, 2024
Accepted Captain Cody Martin's full-time resignation, changing to part-time firefighter/paramedic at $19.50/hour effective April 1, 2024
Accepted firefighter/paramedic Emily's full-time resignation, changing to part-time at $19.50/hour effective April 2, 2024
Changed volunteer firefighter EMT Jack Pegram to part-time status at $16/hour effective April 7, 2024
Authorized issuance of RFP for construction manager for Station 18
Cancelled November 5, 2024 trustee meeting (Election Day) and rescheduled to November 12 at 6 PM
Tabled motion to decrease insured blanket property values pending further investigation
Authorized staff to negotiate lower IT costs with a new company and sign related paperwork
Approved out-of-town travel for Detective McAllister for instructional skills training at Ohio Peace Officers Training Academy, April 8–19, registration cost $1,000
Adjourned into executive session at approximately 7:24 PM per ORC 121.22(G)(1)
action_items: Chief Myers to contact CBIZ appraiser to determine how they arrived at the $1.3 million valuation for Station 19
Administrator Rhoads and Chief Myers to investigate insurance appraisal discrepancies and report back to the board
Administrator Rhoads and Melinda to negotiate with new IT company for lower costs and report back
Fire department and trustees to prepare fire levy for November ballot
Chief Myers to potentially contact Conger for an independent appraisal of Station 19
key_amounts: $19.50/hour – part-time pay rate for Captain Cody Martin and firefighter/paramedic Emily
$16.00/hour – part-time pay rate for Jack Pegram
$1,000 – registration cost for Detective McAllister's training
$19,000/year – potential IT cost savings with new company
$143,000 – current insurance coverage for salt storage (appraised at $50,000)
$228,000 – current insurance for service garage (appraised at $405,000, underinsured by $176,000)
$5,500,000 – current insurance for administration building (appraised at $5,860,000)
$4,000,000 – current insurance for Station 19 (appraised at only $1,300,000 by CBIZ)
$1,700,000–$1,900,000 – insurance company's own evaluation range for Station 19
$460/sq ft – estimated current construction cost per square foot for fire stations
notable_topics: Fire levy failure and plans for November ballot
Loss of full-time firefighter/paramedics due to uncompetitive pay
RFP for construction manager for Station 18
Insurance property appraisal discrepancies, especially Station 19 valuation
Rescheduling November meeting due to Election Day
notes: The meeting opened with a prayer referencing the Baltimore bridge collapse that morning. There was notable frustration about losing firefighter/paramedics to better-paying departments, with trustees expressing concern more departures are coming. The fire levy failed by a small margin and must be placed on the November ballot as it expires in December. Significant concern was raised about the CBIZ insurance appraisal for Station 19, which valued it at $1.3 million despite costing over $2.3 million to build and an estimated $4+ million replacement cost. The board entered executive session regarding personnel matters. There was some procedural confusion at the start regarding the order of agenda adoption and bill payment.
title: BOT 3-26-24
